prs.title: [DOC] fix forecasters horizon and to to predict typos, closes #10116

labels.name: module:forecasting
labels.description: forecasting module: forecasting, incl probabilistic and hierarchical forecasting

prs.body:
## Description

Fixes #10116

### What was wrong
Two typos were present in `fh` parameter docstrings across multiple files:
- `forecasters horizon` → should be `forecasting horizon`
- `to to predict` → should be `to predict` (double word)

### What was changed
Fixed both typos in 58 files across the `sktime/forecasting/` directory.
No logic or functionality was changed — purely docstring fixes.

### How to verify
```bash
grep -rn "forecasters horizon" sktime/  # should return empty
grep -rn "to to predict" sktime/        # should return empty
```

prs.title: Fix/13989 document format validation

labels.name: UX
labels.description: User experience and general design-related items

prs.body:
Fixes #13989

### Description
This PR builds on top of PR #13987 and should be reviewed after it.

No equivalent behaviour existed for document uploads compared to 
`WagtailImageField`. This PR introduces the same UX pattern for 
`WagtailDocumentField` by:
- Adding `get_allowed_document_extensions()` utility function that reads `WAGTAILDOCS_EXTENSIONS` from settings
- Adding `__init__` to `WagtailDocumentField` with dynamic `help_text` showing supported formats and max upload size
- Setting user-friendly `error_messages` for invalid extensions and oversized files
- Adding `check_document_file_format()` to validate uploaded file extensions
- Updating `to_python` to call `check_document_file_format`
- Adding comprehensive tests in `test_document_field.py`
- Updating `CHANGELOG.txt`

### AI usage
None

prs.title: Fix: Login loop and missing name on dashboard (#874)
prs.body:
Fixes #874

## What does this fix?
There was a bug where logging in would trap you in an infinite loop between the login page and the dashboard. This PR fixes that so you can log in smoothly!

## Changes
1. **Stopped the Loop:** Removed some old code that was accidentally forcing you back to the login page even when you were already logged in.
2. **Showed Your Name:** The dashboard now properly says "Welcome, [Your Name]" instead of just "Welcome, User".

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

* **New Features**
  * Added a personalized header greeting showing "Welcome" and the user's name; guest users see a dedicated greeting (e.g., "Guest Pilot").

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Simplified authentication guard behavior to avoid redirect issues.
  * More robust detection and parsing of session/local storage for reliable user name and session state, with improved error handling.

* **Chores**

prs.title: fix: add bottom margin to Resume Mission button
prs.body: Fixes #431 This PR fixes the spacing issue in the dashboard hero section by adding proper bottom margin to the “Resume Mission” button. Previously, the button was visually colliding with the Progress / Streak / Status cards below, making the layout feel cramped and breaking the visual hierarchy. With this change, the button is clearly separated from the stats section and properly grouped within the hero block. This improves layout clarity and makes the primary call-to-action stand out as intended. Screenshots Before After

prs.title: Refactor: Replace jQuery form serialization with encodeForm in privacy-switch
prs.body:
### Summary

This PR removes the jQuery-based `serialize()` implementation from the
`privacy-switch` admin entrypoint and replaces it with a new shared utility
`encodeForm(form)`, which uses FormData + URLSearchParams.

Fixes #13606.

### What changed

- Added `client/src/utils/encodeForm.js`
- Added unit tests for `encodeForm`
- Updated `privacy-switch.js` to use `encodeForm`
- Added full test coverage for the updated `privacy-switch` entrypoint

### Testing

All new tests pass:

- `encodeForm.test.js`
- `privacy-switch.test.js`

prs.title: Fix/inlinepanel min num defaults

labels.name: status:Needs Info
labels.description: Needs more information from the person who reported the issue or opened the PR

prs.body: ### **Fix: InlinePanel `min_num=1` validation fails when default values are present** **Fixes:** #13546 This PR resolves an issue where an `InlinePanel` configured with `min_num=1` and default values would incorrectly fail validation when saving **without making any changes**. Wagtail treated the unchanged form as “not submitted” because the formset reported `has_changed() = False`, triggering the *“Submit at least 1 form”* validation error. --- ## **What Was the Bug?** When a related model had: * default values * `InlinePanel(..., min_num=1, max_num=1)` And the user opened the settings page and clicked **Save** *without editing anything*, Wagtail raised: > **"Submit at least 1 form"** Even though a valid default row was already present. --- ## **What This PR Does** ### ✔ Adds formset-level logic to prevent ignoring default unchanged forms A small mixin ensures default inline forms count toward `min_num`: ```python def should_ignore(self, form): return hasattr(form, "cleaned_data") and not form.has_changed() ``` ### ✔ Introduces `BaseFormSetWithFormTracking` Used by Wagtail admin formsets so that unchanged forms with default values are not dropped during validation. ### ✔ Adds a regression test `wagtail/tests/test_inlinepanel_defaults.py` verifies: * InlinePanel auto-creates a default form * Saving without modifications succeeds * No unwanted validation errors occur --- ## **Screenshot: Working After the Fix** Saving unchanged default fields now works correctly: ---

prs.title: Fix/oembed keyerror
prs.body:
This PR fixes a bug in the **oEmbed Finder** that caused a `KeyError: 'type'` when embedding hidden, deleted, or unauthorized tweets (e.g., 403 responses from Twitter’s oEmbed API).

The fix improves error handling by:

* Catching network and HTTP-related errors (`RequestException`)
* Handling invalid JSON responses gracefully (`ValueError`)
* Checking for missing `"type"` fields in oEmbed responses
* Returning a proper `EmbedNotFoundException` instead of crashing

---

### ✅ **Result**

Now, when an embed URL (like a hidden or deleted tweet) returns an invalid or incomplete response,
Wagtail handles it smoothly and shows:

> “Cannot find an embed for this URL.”

instead of raising a 500 server error.

---

### 🧠 **Type of Change**

* [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
* [ ] New feature
* [ ] Documentation update

---

### 🧪 **How It Was Tested**

* Tested locally on Wagtail development setup
* Tried both:

  * ✅ **Valid embed link** → renders correctly
  * 🚫 **Invalid/hidden tweet link** → shows the fallback message

All tests passed successfully (`pytest` and `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=wagtail.test.settings`).

---

### 🎥 **Verification Video**

> The following video demonstrates the issue resolved:
> * Before fix: Wagtail crashed with a `KeyError: 'type'` when embedding hidden/deleted tweets.
> * After fix: It now gracefully handles such cases and displays the fallback message.


>https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d2a97a8-a044-4e9c-9801-307f37d4bdcf


>https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a1361bf-33df-4b73-8639-a239f6dbcbac

---

### 🏷️ **Related Issue**

Fixes: #13524

issues.title: Live Demo link returns 404 on Netlify for Next.js project
issues.body: The Live Demo button for the Leetcode Clone project points to a URL that returns a Netlify 404 error. This happens because the project is built with Next.js routing, but the deployed Netlify build does not contain the target route. Netlify only serves static files unless configured with the Next.js adapter or static export. As a result, clicking Live Demo leads to a broken link instead of the actual app. This makes the demo unreachable and defeats the purpose of the button. Expected: Live Demo opens a working deployed app. Actual: Live Demo opens a Netlify “Page not found” screen.

issues.title: Live demo link for Food Delivery App (Day 35) is broken
issues.body: The live demo link for Food Delivery App – Day 35 is currently not working. Clicking on the Live Demo button redirects to a Netlify 404 Page Not Found instead of loading the deployed project. This makes it impossible to view or test the Day 35 app from the projects page. The demo URL likely needs to be updated to point to the correct Netlify deployment. I’ve attached screenshots showing the project card and the Netlify 404 page for reference.

issues.title: Resume Mission button has no bottom margin — overlaps with dashboard cards
issues.body: Issue Description The “Resume Mission” button in the hero section does not have enough bottom margin, causing it to visually clash with the cards below it (Progress / Streak / Status). On large screens this creates a cramped, unbalanced layout where the button feels stuck to the stats section instead of being part of the hero block. This breaks visual hierarchy and makes the CTA look like it belongs to the card row instead of the main hero area. Screenshot Please see the screenshot below showing the “Resume Mission” button touching the cards without proper spacing: This highlights how the button visually collides with the Progress / Streak / Status cards, making the hero section feel cramped and misaligned.
